ops.title: Agent-plane delegation review verdicts and accepted risks (2026-07-20)
ops.body:
Finder + adversarial-refuter review (opus ×2, fable synthesis; codex quota-blocked) over the delegation/addressing change set — cc-present 37c1fcb8cc5c, bc5452247630, 0ce491628f0e, 9395134c2e68, f6156f6f9628, 67bc6ccf5102 + cc-interact bec2ae9ae3b2 (v0.13.0, sibling repo).

**Fixed forward** (same day): reducers skip the substrate's `agent.*` namespace in all three ports (smoke-found: registering a handler 500'd every later interaction); `direct` sole-agent resolution filters to present-handler types (was: could inject operator steering into an unrelated subagent via the global agent-inject drain); `outcomes --block` narrows annotations + triage (was: leaked all blocks' entries); handler reconcile keys owed-vs-done on board markers (card `status: redrafted`, reply threads).

**Confirmed sound**: presence keying (kill-9'd child un-mutes ≤ ~90s after its park drops; worst case ≈ the 4-min await timeout on a half-open conn); mute scope (only subscribed types, only the "channel" consumer, cursor advances — no backlog replay on lapse, though a reconnect during lapse can re-deliver the muted tail since the last delivered SSE id, prior-art-consistent); round bookkeeping (child upsert restamps the card; closed-round children correctly re-actionable — flip side: one child write "un-closes" the card's untouched siblings, by design); echo-loop exclusion; TS immutability / Swift value semantics.

**Accepted risks** (deliberate): single-handler serialization is prose-enforced pending the substrate guard (see backlog task 029e202b); a live-but-negligent handler that parks without acting holds the mute indefinitely (presence = liveness-of-await, not of action); replay of pre-child-addressing logs renders child-id removals / after=child inserts as asked rather than as the old reducer no-op'd them (documented in contract.md; boards are short-lived).
